summary.name: The Kinetic Titan
summary.author: Naitik Gupta
summary.description: The strategy is a single position tactical rotation across two high beta leveraged equity ETFs, selecting the asset with the strongest recent risk adjusted momentum and otherwise moving defensively into cash like bills. Each day it computes a 21 trading day momentum signal for TQQQ (levered Nasdaq 100) and ERX (levered US energy equities) and scales that return by the assets recent daily return volatility to form a momentum per unit risk score. The portfolio holds only the top scored risk asset
summary.description_full:
The strategy is a single position tactical rotation across two high beta leveraged equity ETFs, selecting the asset with the strongest recent risk adjusted momentum and otherwise moving defensively into cash like bills. Each day it computes a 21 trading day momentum signal for TQQQ (levered Nasdaq 100) and ERX (levered US energy equities) and scales that return by the assets recent daily return volatility to form a momentum per unit risk score. The portfolio holds only the top scored risk asset when its score is positive; if both scores are negative it allocates fully to BIL as the capital preservation sleeve. This targets short horizon trend persistence while attempting to avoid whipsaws in regime shifts via risk normalization.

Rebalancing decisions are made once per day near the close using daily history; intraday minute data is used for execution only. Switching is constrained by a hysteresis buffer: it will rotate from the current risk asset to the challenger only if the challenger score exceeds the current score by 0.05, and it will not re risk unless a positive score re emerges. Position sizing uses volatility targeting: the chosen risk asset weight equals 40 percent divided by its annualized 20 day realized volatility, capped at 100 percent, with any residual allocated to BIL; holdings are adjusted only when the weight drift exceeds 5 percent and all non target positions are liquidated.

raw.name: The Kinetic Titan

author.name: Naitik Gupta
author.bio: High school student exploring cool ideas in systematic trading, risk, and markets.

raw.description:

The portfolio holds only the top scored risk asset when its score is positive; if both scores are negative it allocates fully to BIL as the capital preservation sleeve. This targets short horizon trend persistence while attempting to avoid whipsaws in regime shifts via risk normalization.

Rebalancing decisions are made once per day near the close using daily history; intraday minute data is used for execution only. Switching is constrained by a hysteresis buffer: it will rotate from the current risk asset to the challenger only if the challenger score exceeds the current score by 0.05, and it will not re risk unless a positive score re emerges.
